expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the actionability question in JSON and Markdown
report-quality emits a fixtureCandidates entry for the public synthetic case
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
the fixture exposes markerVisibilityReview.summary counts for total, visible, ceiling, missing-ceiling, upgrade-trigger, missing-trigger, upgrade-status, and omitted marker rows
the fixture exposes markerVisibilityReview.visibilityRows for tracked and needs-trigger shortcuts
the fixture keeps every shortcut marker location, ceiling, and upgrade-trigger status visible without claiming benchmark savings
the Markdown exposes Marker Visibility Review with the same shortcut rows before the raw ledger table
fixtureCandidate.privateDataPolicy: This materialized starter is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly remain explicit.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.
Register the fixture in validation only after the report-quality check passes.

fixtureCandidate.publicFixtureRecipe: Create a minimal synthetic ShipGuard report pair that reproduces this report-quality question without private app code, local paths, screenshots, customer data, app names, or proprietary source snippets.
fixtureCandidate.sourceQuestion: Does Lean Debt make every shortcut marker visible with a ceiling and upgrade trigger?

leanDebtLedger.description: Synthetic shortcut markers for standalone Lean Debt marker-visibility coverage.

markers.summary: use the native query parser while this bridge handles one query shape. ceiling: one query shape. upgrade: replace when repeated-key support is required.
markers.upgrade: replace when repeated-key support is required

markers.summary: keep the temporary compatibility panel during migration. ceiling: one release migration window.

markerVisibilityReview.policy: Every intentional shortcut marker should be rendered as a row with location, summary, ceiling, upgrade-trigger status, and explicit missing-trigger state when the upgrade is not yet written.

visibilityRows.summary: use the native query parser while this bridge handles one query shape. ceiling: one query shape. upgrade: replace when repeated-key support is required.
visibilityRows.upgradeTrigger: replace when repeated-key support is required

visibilityRows.summary: keep the temporary compatibility panel during migration. ceiling: one release migration window.

nextActions: Add an upgrade trigger to every needs-trigger marker.
Remove markers whose ceiling no longer applies.
Use shipguard lean review on active diffs so new shortcuts are tracked before merge.
reportQualityQuestions: Does Lean Debt make every shortcut marker visible with a ceiling and upgrade trigger?

scanScope.note: Synthetic fixture; no private source tree was scanned.

scopeBoundary.purpose: Synthetic public fixture for ShipGuard report-quality behavior.

sourceInfluence.boundary: ShipGuard implements a native shortcut ledger for its own lean-code markers. It does not vendor Ponytail code.
sourceInfluence.name: Ponytail
